summary: Promote the existing in-code dual-AC fixture (__tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts:98-241) to a frozen on-disk fixture under src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/dual-ac-frozen/. Add anti-tautology guard. Verify CI wiring is blocking. Three atomic commits.

seed_test.summary: AC-05 dual-identity test that builds a 3-file × 3-node fixture procedurally inside the test (NUM_FILES=3, NODES_PER_FILE=3, MISSING_PER_FILE=2). Writes graph.json + 3 source files into a tempdir per test run. Asserts: missingSkips.length === 6 (AC-05a), uniqueFiles.size === 3 (AC-05b), missingSkips.length !== uniqueFiles.size (distinctness).
seed_test.weakness: Inputs are built procedurally from JS constants → not diffable as a PR artifact, not inspectable as a frozen baseline, vulnerable to silent test-helper drift if the construction code changes shape. Expected counts (6, 3) are hard-coded numbers in expectations — borderline tautology since the same constants compute both the input and the expectation.
seed_test.strength: The shape (3×3 with 2 missing + 1 defined per file) correctly exercises both grains and produces distinct numbers (6 ≠ 3) — this is the right shape; only the storage form changes.

ci_wiring_baseline.vitest_config: Default vitest config picks up __tests__/pipeline/* per current setup — verify in this WO
ci_wiring_baseline.blocking_status: Test is blocking today (vitest run fails the test if assertions fail) — verify in this WO
ci_wiring_baseline.package_json_script: Default `npm test` or equivalent runs vitest — verify

fixture_target_location.rationale: Per dispatch-045 boundary directive (src/integration/rag/__tests__/* + minimal CI wiring). The seed test currently sits at __tests__/pipeline/, but the frozen fixture is fundamentally a rag-integration artifact — co-locating it with the rag tests improves cohesion. The test that uses the fixture lives at src/integration/rag/__tests__/dual-ac-frozen.test.ts (new) OR refactor the existing __tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts AC-05 block to load the fixture (decision in plan).

investigation_targets.rationale: Seed test exists and is the working reference; surface area is fully enumerated. The plan handles a single decision point (refactor existing test vs author new test) inline, not via a phase.

implementation_strategy.approach: Three atomic commits: (C1) author frozen fixture + README; (C2) author or refactor test to load the fixture, with anti-tautology guard; (C3) CI wiring verification + lock-down.

commits.label: C1: author frozen fixture
operations: Author dual-ac-frozen/graph.json: serialize the 9-node graph (3 files × 3 nodes; 2 hs=missing + 1 hs=defined per file). Identical shape to what the seed test currently builds in code, but as a static file.
Author 3 source files dual-ac-frozen/src/mod0.ts, mod1.ts, mod2.ts each with `export function fnN()`, `export class ClsN`, `export const CN`. Identical to seed.

Author dual-ac-frozen/README.md: documents the invariant — skipped element count === graph nodes with hs=missing; uniqueFiles(skipDetails).size === validation_report.header_missing_count. Documents that 2415/263 are real-codebase anchors and NOT load-bearing for this fixture.
commits.tsc_check: tsc --noEmit clean both configs (no .ts changes here, but verify nothing breaks)

commits.label: C2: load frozen fixture in test + anti-tautology guard
commits.scope: Either __tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts (refactor existing AC-05 block) OR src/integration/rag/__tests__/dual-ac-frozen.test.ts (new file). Plan recommends the refactor path — keep the assertions where they were when shipped; replace the procedural build with a frozen-fixture load.
operations: Replace the in-code build block (lines ~104-186 in current seed) with: copy fixture dir into a tmpdir (or read directly from on-disk fixture path), read graph.json + validation-report.json from the fixture

Anti-tautology guard: expected element-grain count is computed from the loaded graph.json (count of nodes with metadata.headerStatus==='missing'), NOT from a hardcoded literal 6 in the test. Same for file-grain (size of unique files in the same node set). If the fixture changes shape later, the expectation tracks; the test still asserts identity, not number.
Distinctness assertion preserved: expected element-grain !== expected file-grain (compute both from the input independently; assert the relation)
Verify the test file still compiles (tsc) and runs green (vitest)

commits.label: C3: CI wiring verification + lock-down
commits.scope: package.json scripts and/or vitest.config.ts; possibly a CI gate script
operations: Verify default `vitest run` picks up the dual-AC test (whether refactored in __tests__/pipeline/ or new at src/integration/rag/__tests__/)
Verify the test is blocking (failed assertions return non-zero exit)
If anything is unclear or advisory-only today, lock it down: explicit include glob for the fixture's test file, package.json `test` script that runs vitest with the right config
Document the lock-down in the WO communication.json so the close summary captures the before/after

verification: Refactored test (or new test) runs green with the frozen fixture
Mutate the fixture (e.g., flip a single node from hs=missing to hs=defined) and confirm the test FAILS — proves the assertion is real, not tautological. Revert the mutation before commit.
Full `vitest run` pass on the rag-related test suites
Real-codebase anchor still intact: predecessor archive's verify-dual-ac.mjs static probe still reports 2415===2415 + 263===263 (sanity check, optional)

risks.description: Refactoring the existing __tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts AC-05 block introduces a regression in the gate-invariant tests (a) (b) (c) that share the same file
risks.mitigation: The AC-05 block is structurally separate from the (a)/(b)/(c) Phase 7 chokepoint INVARIANT block — they live in different `describe` blocks. Edits are confined to the AC-05 describe block.

risks.description: Frozen fixture's graph.json drift from the schema rag-index expects (e.g., a future schema change to graph.json node shape)
risks.impact: fixture becomes stale and the test fails for the wrong reason
risks.mitigation: Document the fixture invariant in README.md; add a comment in the test pointing to the README; if a future schema change happens, the fixture is updated as part of that schema change, not silently

risks.description: Anti-tautology guard is implemented incorrectly — expected count is still effectively computed from runtime output
risks.impact: the WO ships but the invariant is vulnerable
risks.mitigation: Plan calls out: expected count comes from inspecting the loaded fixture file (the input), NOT from inspecting the IndexingResult (the output). The test computes expected element-grain count by reading graph.json and counting hs=missing nodes; computes expected file-grain count by reading graph.json and counting unique files among hs=missing nodes. These come from the input. The IndexingResult is what's compared against.

risks.description: CI wiring is already correct and the WO produces no actionable C3 change
risks.impact: C3 commit is small or empty
risks.mitigation: If verification confirms current wiring is correct + blocking, document the verification in C3's commit message and skip code changes. The verification itself is the value of the commit.

expected_outcome.loc_delta: +30 to +80 net LOC (new fixture files + minor test refactor) — modest positive, expected per dispatch-045 blast radius
expected_outcome.files_changed: 5-7 files (3 fixture sources + graph.json + validation-report.json + README + 1 test refactor; possibly +1 CI config)
expected_outcome.test_pass_rate: 100% — refactored test still green; existing tests preserved
